videoLog.title: Why airport lounges lose money on purpose
videoLog.hookText: Ever wonder why an airport lounge hands you free champagne it clearly loses money on? There's a reason, and it isn't generosity.

videoLog.title: What a vending machine really costs to run
videoLog.hookText: A vending machine is a definition of passive income: a box that sells snacks while you sleep. It is a coin-operated retail unit.

videoLog.title: How does a casino guarantee it wins?
videoLog.hookText: How does a casino guarantee it wins, every single night, no matter who is at the table? The answer is hidden in the carpet.

videoLog.title: The supply chain behind a banana
videoLog.hookText: The banana is the world's most popular fruit. It is a tropical berry grown in over 100 countries and shipped green.

hypotheses.pattern: Opening with a curiosity-gap question that names a mystery and promises a hidden reason sustains early retention; flat declarative/definitional openers collapse in the first 10%.
Question-style mystery hooks also correlate with higher CTR (~0.08 vs ~0.03), suggesting the title/hook framing carries both click and hold.

appliedRules.rule: Opening with a curiosity-gap question that names a mystery and promises a hidden reason sustains early retention; flat declarative/definitional openers collapse in the first 10%.
appliedRules.rationale: Promoted from hypothesis on sufficient evidence.

appliedRules.rule: Open every script with a curiosity-gap question that names a specific mystery and explicitly promises a hidden/non-obvious reason. Never open with a flat definitional statement (e.g., 'X is a...').
appliedRules.rationale: Both videos using question/mystery hooks held ~0.92-0.94 retention at the 10% mark and finished above 0.40; both videos using definitional openers dropped to ~0.60 by 10% and below 0.20 by the end.

dropoffNotes.pattern: Scripts that open with a definitional/declarative statement lose ~40% of viewers within the first 10% of runtime, vs ~6-8% loss for question-hook scripts.
dropoffNotes.mappedTo: Replace definitional cold opens with an unresolved curiosity-gap question; defer definitions until after the hook lands.

changelog.change: Created hypothesis hyp-curiosity-hook and promoted it to an applied rule on hook shape.
changelog.why: Clean 2-vs-2 structural contrast: mystery-question hooks held early retention and finished strong; definitional hooks cratered in the first 10%.

changelog.change: Logged hyp-ctr-hook-correlation as an unpromoted hypothesis.
changelog.why: CTR co-moves with hook style but click behavior is confounded by title/thumbnail; insufficient isolation to rule-ify.

changelog.change: Added 0-10% dropoff note mapping early crater to definitional cold opens.
changelog.why: The retention loss is concentrated structurally at the open, not spread across the video.
